title: primula veris subsp. officinalis
intro: Primula veris subsp. officinalis, also known as primrose, is a subspecies of the primula veris plant, a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Asia. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help relieve coughs, colds, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of primula veris subsp. officinalis include its ability to reduce fever, soothe sore throats, and alleviate digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas, syrups, or added to topical creams and ointments.
